title: Quantification of HK2 Protein in Mouse Samples Using ELISA
problem: Accurate quantification of HK2 protein in mouse samples (serum, tissue) is essential for metabolic studies. Manual ELISA procedures are time-consuming, error-prone, and lack consistency. Automation ensures precision, scalability, and reproducibility in high-throughput experiments.
abstract: The experiment involves using ELISA to quantify HK2 protein levels in mouse samples. A 96-well plate is prepared with standard dilutions, samples, and controls. Reagents such as NADH standard, detection buffer, and reaction mix are dispensed using an automated liquid handling system. The plate is incubated, and absorbance is measured at 450 nm using a microplate reader. The results provide a quantitative assessment of HK2 levels.
description: The lab setting includes an automated liquid handling system (Biomek i7 Hybrid, Span8+MC96), an automated refrigerator (Cytomat 2C425), and a microplate reader (SpectraMax i3x). Reagents and samples are prepared according to the ELISA protocol, with specific volumes and dilutions defined. The workflow involves standard curve preparation, sample dilution, and reaction setup, followed by incubation and detection.
query: I need to perform an ELISA to quantify HK2 protein in mouse serum samples. Please prepare the standard curve, dilute the samples, and set up the reaction mix using the automated liquid handler. Ensure the correct volumes and incubation conditions are followed.
